method_atom: a universality mechanism in which an effective low-dimensional (two-dimensional) conformal symmetry emerging at a distinguished boundary or interface of a system so strongly constrains the counting of microscopic states that a macroscopic statistical quantity is fixed independent of the microscopic details of the underlying theory; the symmetry is broken by an anomaly, yielding Goldstone-like collective excitations that carry the relevant degrees of freedom
object_atom: black holes and their event horizons in a quantum theory of gravity, specifically the statistical-mechanical origin of black hole thermodynamic entropy and thermal behavior
brief_reason: the anomaly-broken conformal-symmetry universal state-counting machinery is field-neutral and transferable, while the black-hole horizon and its entropy in quantum gravity is the domain-specific object.
